frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: real-time fMRI multivoxel pattern reinforcement to manipulate targeted neural representations

frameworks.name: metacognition
frameworks.one_line: subjective confidence in perception treated as dissociable from perceptual accuracy

frameworks.name: frontoparietal network of confidence
frameworks.one_line: frontoparietal multivoxel patterns posited as substrate of perceptual confidence

frameworks.name: dissociation of confidence and performance
frameworks.one_line: challenges accounts that tie subjective confidence directly to perceptual sensitivity

core_question: Can manipulating frontoparietal multivoxel activity patterns modulate perceptual confidence independently of perceptual performance?
key_assumptions: multivoxel patterns in frontoparietal cortex encode perceptual confidence
DecNef can selectively bias targeted patterns
confidence and perceptual performance are separable processes
manipulations: direction of induced multivoxel pattern change in frontoparietal network via DecNef
measurement of perceptual confidence reports vs. perceptual accuracy
key_findings: DecNef of frontoparietal patterns modulates perceptual confidence
perceptual performance is unaffected by the same manipulation
confidence and performance dissociate under targeted pattern induction
interpretations: frontoparietal network plays a distinct role in metacognition separate from perceptual processing
subjective confidence and perceptual sensitivity rely on dissociable neural substrates
